plant.description: Unique History: A highly sacred and valued tree of India, often called the 'Nirgundi' or 'Five-leaved Chaste Tree'. Mentioned in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita for its unmatched 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-destroying) and 'Vranaropana' (wound-purifying) properties. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in classical Sanskrit literature as the 'Chaste Tree'. Physical Characteristics: A small-to-medium-sized deciduous shrub reaching up to 5-8 meters in height, with quadrangular stems and opposite, 3-to-5-foliate (palmate), green leaves. The flowers are attractive, bluish-purple, in terminal panicles. The fruit is a small, globose drupe (black when ripe) containing a single seed. It is found throughout India and tropical/sub-tropical regions worldwide. It is widely cultivated globally for medicinal and ornamental use. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Iridoid Glycosides (Agnuside), Flavonoids (Vitexoside), and Essential Oils. The presence of agnuside (0.5-2.0%) is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-tubercular effects (when Appropriately diluted). Strict Standardizations: The leaf and root are standardized in API based on its greenish-grey color and characteristic strong-sweet-musky-bitter taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<8%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPLC is used to monitor 1% to 2% agnuside in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Vata and Kapha Hara (pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas). Due to its Katu (pungent) and Tikta (bitter) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-relieving) and 'Vatavyadhi' (neurological disorders) therapies, clearing the cold, heavy, and sticky qualities of Kapha while balancing the dry and rough qualities of Vata through its systemic warming and penetrating property. It does not aggravate Pitta when used appropriately.

leaf_juice__swarasa_.method: 10-20ml of fresh leaf juice taken twice daily on an empty stomach for maximum pain relief and detoxification.

nirgundi_taila.method: Medicated oil preparation used primarily for local application on the joints (pain-relief) and scalp (alopecia).

leaf_powder__churna_.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with milk or honey for managing chronic joint pain and fever.

root_decoction__kwatha_.method: 20g of root boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic Vata issues and fevers. Take twice daily before meals.

external_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh leaf or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, joint pain, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Swarasa (Juice): 10-20 ml; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Taila: 1-2 drops (internal, with general precautions), divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining joint health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: World-Famous Analgesic and Anti-inflammatory Specialist: Nirgundi is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of musculoskeletal pain and arthritis. Its 'Tikshna' (sharp) and 'Ushna' (heating) nature provided by iridoid glycosides helps in providing instant relief from joint pain, muscle stiffness, and sciatica. WHO-aligned research confirms its potent anti-inflammatory and systemic analgesic properties.

Neurological and Sciatica support: Known as 'Vedanasthapana' (analgesic). It effectively reduces the intensity and frequency of nerve pain (Gridhrasi) and chronic Vata-related nerve disorders by providing systemic relief from 'Vata-avarana' (Vata-obstruction) in the nerve channels.

Respiratory and Cough support: Its Ushna (heating) potency helps in liquefying and expelling thick mucus from the lungs through its Tikshna-Katu (sharp-pungent) action. It is traditionally used in 'Kasa' (cough) and 'Shwasa' (asthma) when there is excess Kapha accumulation.

Skin Health and Wound Healing: Its antimicrobial and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions like acne, psoriasis, and eczema. The leaf juice applied topically promotes rapid relief from acne and sun-burns while providing systemic 'Vranaropana' (wound-healing) effects.

Antipyretic and Chronic Fever: Highly effective as a 'Jwarahara' (fever-reliever), particularly in persistent and malarial fevers. It helps in reducing the intensity and duration of fevers by clearing systemic 'Ama' (toxins). It is a key herb for managing recovery from viral infections like dengue.

Worm and Internal Parasite Care: Its extremely bitter nature makes it incomparable for managing stubborn intestinal parasites and worms (Krimighna) that thrive in a high-Kapha environment. It helps in cleared stagnant toxins from the gut.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Iridoid Glycosides (Agnuside, Negundoside), Flavonoids (Casticin, Vitexin), and Essential Oils. Agnuside and Negundoside are the primary bioactives responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory, anti-carcinogenic, and anti-tubercular action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cultures and traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with extreme high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Avoid during pregnancy in very large therapeutic doses except under professional guidance.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness. Discontinue if extreme bitterness causes nausea.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-hypertensive or anti-diabetic medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
